de.uka.ilkd.key.proof

Class NodeInfo

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.NodeInfo

  • public class NodeInfo
extends java.lang.Object
    The node info object contains additional information about a node used to give user feedback. The content does not have any influence on the proof or carry something of logical value.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      NodeInfo(Node node)

      returns the active statement of the JavaBlock the applied rule has been matched against or null if no rule has been applied yet or the applied rule was no taclet or program transformation rule

      returns the branch label

      returns the name of the source file where the active statement occurs or the string NONE if the statement does not originate from a source file (e.g.

      returns the position of the executed statement in its source code or Position.UNDEFINED

      returns a string representation of the first statement or null if no such exists

      Get user-provided plain-text annotations.

      Returns a set containing URIs of all files relevant to this node.

      returns true if the rule applied on this node has been performed by a proof script command.

      • NodeInfo

        public NodeInfo(Node node)

    • Method Detail

      • computeActiveStatement

        public static SourceElement computeActiveStatement(RuleApp ruleApp)

        Computes the active statement in the given RuleApp.

        This functionality is independent from concrete NodeInfos and used for instance by the symbolic execution tree extraction.

        Parameters:
        ruleApp - The given RuleApp.
        Returns:
        The active statement or null if no one is provided.

      • computeFirstStatement

        public static SourceElement computeFirstStatement(RuleApp ruleApp)

        Computes the first statement in the given RuleApp.

        This functionality is independent from concrete NodeInfos and used for instance by the symbolic execution tree extraction.

        Parameters:
        ruleApp - The given RuleApp.
        Returns:
        The first statement or null if no one is provided.

      • computeActiveStatement

        public static SourceElement computeActiveStatement(SourceElement firstStatement)

        Computes the active statement in the given SourceElement.

        This functionality is independent from concrete NodeInfos and used for instance by the symbolic execution tree extraction.

        Parameters:
        firstStatement - The given SourceElement.
        Returns:
        The active statement or null if no one is provided.

      • isSymbolicExecutionRuleApplied

        public static boolean isSymbolicExecutionRuleApplied(Node node)
        Checks if a rule is applied on the given Node which performs symbolic execution.
        Parameters:
        node - The Node to check.
        Returns:
        true symbolic execution is performed, false otherwise.

      • isSymbolicExecutionRuleApplied

        public static boolean isSymbolicExecutionRuleApplied(RuleApp app)
        Checks if the given RuleApp performs symbolic execution.
        Parameters:
        app - The RuleApp to check.
        Returns:
        true symbolic execution is performed, false otherwise.

      • isSymbolicExecution

        public static boolean isSymbolicExecution(Taclet t)

      • getActiveStatement

        public SourceElement getActiveStatement()
        returns the active statement of the JavaBlock the applied rule has been matched against or null if no rule has been applied yet or the applied rule was no taclet or program transformation rule
        Returns:
        active statement as described above

      • getBranchLabel

        public java.lang.String getBranchLabel()
        returns the branch label
        Returns:
        branch label

      • getExecStatementParentClass

        public java.lang.String getExecStatementParentClass()
        returns the name of the source file where the active statement occurs or the string NONE if the statement does not originate from a source file (e.g. created by a taclet application or part of a generated implicit method)
        Returns:
        name of source file as described above

      • getExecStatementPosition

        public Position getExecStatementPosition()
        returns the position of the executed statement in its source code or Position.UNDEFINED
        Returns:
        statement position as described above

      • getFirstStatementString

        public java.lang.String getFirstStatementString()
        returns a string representation of the first statement or null if no such exists
        Returns:
        string representation of first statement as described above

      • setBranchLabel

        public void setBranchLabel(java.lang.String s)
        sets the branch label of a node. Schema variables occurring in string s are replaced by their instantiations if possible
        Parameters:
        s - the String to be set

      • setInteractiveRuleApplication

        public void setInteractiveRuleApplication(boolean b)
        parameter indicated if the rule has been applied interactively or not
        Parameters:
        b - a boolean indicating interactive application

      • setScriptRuleApplication

        public void setScriptRuleApplication(boolean b)
        parameter indicated if the rule has been applied by a proof script or not
        Parameters:
        b - a boolean indicating scripting application

      • getInteractiveRuleApplication

        public boolean getInteractiveRuleApplication()
        returns true if the rule applied on this node has been performed manually by the user
        Returns:
        boolean for interactive rule application as described above

      • getScriptRuleApplication

        public boolean getScriptRuleApplication()
        returns true if the rule applied on this node has been performed by a proof script command. For rule, macro commands etc., the first node is marked.
        Returns:
        boolean for proof script rule application as described above

      • getRelevantFiles

        public ImmutableSet<java.net.URI> getRelevantFiles()

        Returns a set containing URIs of all files relevant to this node.

        This includes the files contained in the PositionInfo of all modalities as well as the files in the OriginTermLabels of all terms in this node's sequent.

        Returns:
        the set of URIs of files relevant to this node.

      • addRelevantFile

        public void addRelevantFile(java.net.URI relevantFile)
        Add a file to the set returned by getRelevantFiles().
        Parameters:
        relevantFile - the URI of the file to add.

      • addRelevantFiles

        public void addRelevantFiles(ImmutableSet<java.net.URI> relevantFiles)
        Add some files to the set returned by getRelevantFiles().
        Parameters:
        relevantFiles - the URIs of the files to add.

      • setNotes

        public void setNotes(java.lang.String newNotes)
        Add user-provided plain-text annotations.
        Parameters:
        newNotes - annotations as described above

      • getNotes

        public java.lang.String getNotes()
        Get user-provided plain-text annotations.
        Returns:
        annotations as described above

        public SequentChangeInfo getSequentChangeInfo()
